stat_of_week: 8 - Sugar Land Skeeters posted a microscopic 2.11 ERA across 64.0 innings this week, the lowest ERA by a winning team in league history, eclipsing General Hospital's previous mark of 2.1 from Week 2
thriller_note: The two teams dead-locked in a tie, with Buttville's dominant ERA (2.25) canceling out General Hospital's superior pitching volume and RBI production, leaving both squads to split points in a result that stings General Hospital most given their luck index shows they were the most snake-bitten team in the league this week.
key_storyline: Sugar Land Skeeters continued their march toward league dominance, posting a third consecutive top-tier scoring week capped by a 2.11 ERA and a dominating 8–2 victory over the Abbotsford-Colby Twins. Matt Olson (3 HR, 8 RBI, 8 R) and Drake Baldwin led a balanced offensive attack that won six of eight hitting categories. The Skeeters have now finished first or second in total category wins in all three weeks of the season, establishing themselves as the clear team to beat entering Week 4.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers posted the league's highest luck delta (+0.91) this week, earning a win despite generating just 34 strikeouts and a bloated 5.53 ERA from only 42.1 innings pitched — the weakest pitching performance among any team that won this week.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs posted a sterling 4.30 ERA, 1.13 WHIP, 62 strikeouts, 5 quality starts, and 7 NSVH from their pitching staff — better than their opponent in nearly every mound category — yet still fell 5–4, finishing with the league's worst luck delta at -0.60 for the week.
records_broken: The Sugar Land Skeeters set a new league record for lowest ERA by a winning team with a 2.11 mark across 64.0 innings, narrowly breaking the previous record of 2.1 set by General Hospital in Week 2.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Three weeks of elite production, historically low ERA, balanced offense led by Olson and Baldwin — this is the league's best team | up
2. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5-HR explosion anchors a team winning close matchups; pitching staff has quietly been reliable across all three weeks | same
3. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood and Willson Contreras put up absurd numbers (Wood: .636 OBP; Contreras: 11 RBI) in a convincing win; Judge adds elite depth | up
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Flipped the script with an 8–2 blowout, leading the league with 6 QS and strong across-the-board pitching; Bryce Harper (.552 OBP) heating up | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost despite a 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP — elite pitching but bats must produce more; strong underlying metrics keep them in the top half | down
6. General Hospital | Most unlucky team this week; a tie when their expected wins say they should have won outright — talent is real, results haven't followed | down
7. North Correa | Chris Sale's 22-K, 16-IP week is a monster performance and Ohtani remains elite, but offense ranked near the bottom with only 18 runs scored | same
8. Riverside Roughnecks | Manager spotlight week delivered a 6–3 win; Trevor Story's 9 RBI and zero quality starts somehow worked — sustainability is the question | up
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Acuña, Turang, and Sal Stewart all had strong individual weeks but the pitching (5.05 ERA) continues to sink the ship | same
10. Buttville Skidmarks | A tie feels like a loss when your ERA is 2.25; offense needs to wake up — only 22 RBI and 7 HR in a week where runs were scarce | down
11. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Active waiver week (Masyn Winn, Dustin May added) signals urgency; Imanaga was excellent but the offense is a real liability at .306 OBP | up
12. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Got blown out 8–2 despite decent team counting stats; pitching gave up runs in bunches and the Merrill Kelly add needs to pay off fast | down
13. Benchwarmers | Won this week but with the worst pitching profile among winners; 42.1 IP and a 5.53 ERA is not a formula for sustained success | same
14. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson's 5-HR week was spectacular but the team still lost 8–2; getting crushed in pitching categories weekly is a fatal flaw | same
waiver_highlights: General Hospital | B | Added Kumar Rocker (SP); dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk, Cristian Javier | Aggressive rotation overhaul signals the team knows their pitching (5.48 ERA this week) is the obstacle between them and contention — Rocker has upside but is unproven at the MLB level.

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A- | Added Masyn Winn (SS), Dustin May (SP), Scott Barlow (RP); dropped multiple players | The Wonderdogs made the most moves in the league and targeted real contributors — Winn is a legitimate SB source and May adds rotation depth after Imanaga's strong showing.

Albuquerque Bombers | B- | Added Keaton Winn, Braxton Garrett (SPs), Josh Jung, Noelvi Marte (bats); dropped four players | Volume addition with mixed upside — Jung and Marte add lineup flexibility but neither reliever adds certainty to a rotation that posted a 5.05 ERA this week.

North Correa | C+ | Added Austin Wells (C), Brayan Rocchio (2B/SS); dropped J.T. Realmuto, Brooks Lee | Replacing Realmuto with Wells is a lateral move at best and a downgrade at worst; Rocchio offers speed but limited upside at a crowded middle infield position.

Riverside Roughnecks | B | Added Victor Vodnik (RP); dropped Mark Vientos | Vientos was a source of offensive upside, but with zero quality starts this week, shoring up the bullpen for saves and holds is the right priority call.
